blocks.content:
You can configure panels, rows, and tabs to be shown or hidden based on rules. For example, you can set a panel to be hidden if there’s no data returned by a query or a tab to only be shown if a specific variable value is present.



Another type of rule is a time-based rule. For example, you might have panels that are only useful during an incident, so you've configured them to be hidden most of the time. As you narrow the dashboard's time range to investigate a spike, those panels automatically appear to provide more detailed insights. This keeps dashboards focused by showing time-sensitive panels only when they're relevant to the selected time range.



In this guide, you'll learn how to configure a show/hide rule that displays a panel only when the dashboard time range is one hour or less.
Create a new dashboard by clicking **New**, then selecting **New dashboard**.
Hover your cursor over the panel.
Open the data source selector, search for `-- Gra`, and select the **-- Grafana --** data source.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='input-wrapper'] input[data-testid='data-testid Select a data source']

blocks.content:
Set the dashboard layout to **Auto**. 

Your dashboard needs to be in auto layout to use show/hide rules so that it can dynamically reorganize when panels appear and disappear.
Click the panel header to bring it back into focus. Now the sidebar shows panel configuration options.
Now let's add a rule. Click **Add rule**, then select **Time range less than**.

blocks.reftarget: {grafana:pages.Dashboard.Sidebar.conditionalRendering.timeRange.select} div:has(input[data-testid='data-testid Dashboard Conditional Rendering Time range select-input'])
blocks.content: Set the time range to **1 hour**.

Now, the dashboard only displays this panel if the dashboard time range is 1 hour or less. Since the dashboard time range is currently **Last 6 hours**, the panel now displays the hidden icon and won't be visible in view mode.

To make the panel visible again, let's change the dashboard time range to **Last 1 hour**.

Well done! Now you know:

- The dashboard layout requirements for using show/hide rules.
- How to access find show/hide rules in the dashboard sidebar.
- How to configure a time-based show/hide rule.
